opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My heart keeps skipping beats and fluttering in my chest when I lie down, and I feel like I am going to have a heart attack. I can't sleep, I'm really scared.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started tonight while I was trying to sleep and it won't stop, I'm too anxious to just wait it out.

symptom_narrative.onset: started earlier this evening, gradually, while trying to fall asleep
symptom_narrative.duration: on and off since it started tonight
symptom_narrative.character: a fluttering, skipping feeling, and I'm really aware of my own heartbeat, like I can feel every beat
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really distressing, I feel panicked about it, though I don't have any actual pain
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes and goes, seems worse when I'm lying still trying to sleep
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: not sure anything makes it fully better, though it's a bit less noticeable when I'm distracted
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down and being still, and just focusing on it makes it feel worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no chest pain, no shortness of breath, haven't fainted or felt like I was about to faint, just the fluttering and feeling really anxious
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I don't think this has happened before, not like this anyway
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't sleep because of it and I'm quite worked up about it

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: I've had a lot of caffeine today, a few energy drinks and coffee, more than usual
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've been really stressed lately and not sleeping well

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: genuinely afraid of having a heart attack, wants strong reassurance and definitive proof nothing serious is wrong

communication_style.typical_message_style: writes anxiously, repeats concerns, seeks reassurance
